title: China drives growth in steel flows as the rest of the world sees a small contraction
section_path: China drives growth in steel flows as the rest of the world sees a small contraction
section_path_text: China drives growth in steel flows as the rest of the world sees a small contraction
summary:
Main
Steel flows climb in 2025 as weak domestic demand in China leads to greater reliance on export markets. This trend has continued into 2026. Image reference: unnamed.png | assets/2026-02-17_china-drives-growth-in-steel-flows-as-the-rest-of-the-world-sees-a-small-contrac_img_unnamed_1d02278cecf1.png
Key Takeaways
● Steel flows in 2025 are 5% higher than in 2024.

children.title: Main
children.summary: Steel flows climb in 2025 as weak domestic demand in China leads to greater reliance on export markets. This trend has continued into 2026.

children.title: China's steel exports will drive supramax demand
section_path: China's steel exports will drive supramax demand
children.section_path_text: China's steel exports will drive supramax demand
children.summary: Steel exports make up over 41% of China's dry bulk seaborne exports, according to TSOP. From that figure, 67% is shipped by supramax.
